description: Add day-of-week exclusion for low-edge days. The hour_cos Ã— dow_sin interaction (0.189) is the strongest pairwise interaction in the entire SHAP matrix â€” stronger than any single feature except hour_cos itself. dow_sin = sin(2Ï€Â·dow/7) peaks mid-week (Wed/Thu) and troughs at week boundaries (Mon/Fri-Sun). Gold exhibits known behavioral patterns: Monday is range-establishing (institutional flow re-pricing from weekend gaps), Friday afternoon sees position-squaring ahead of the weekend. The interaction with hour_cos suggests that the London-NY session edge is strongest Tue-Thu and weakest Mon/Fri. Excluding or reducing exposure on Monday and Friday would align with the SHAP evidence and known gold market microstructure.
expected_improvement: Stacking the day-of-week filter on top of the hour window should capture the interaction effect. The interaction SHAP (0.189) is nearly as large as dow_sin alone (0.244), meaning most of the day-of-week signal comes from its interplay with hour â€” confirming that day+hour together matter more than either alone. Expect an additional +2-3pp win rate improvement on top of the hour filter, at the cost of ~40% fewer trading days (losing Mon+Fri). Net: fewer trades but higher quality.
